_meta.description: Country-level demographic grounding data for persona generation. Add new countries by adding a new top-level key. Run scripts/fetch_world_bank.py to refresh World Bank figures.

_sources: DataReportal Digital Philippines 2024 (We Are Social / Meltwater)

income_distribution.description: Approximate share of households per income band (PSA 2023)

top_platforms_ranked: Facebook (highest penetration in Southeast Asia — 96% of social users)

notes: Strong collectivist culture — family and community influence purchase decisions heavily
Brand loyalty is high once trust is established
Value-consciousness: price sensitivity balanced with aspiration for branded goods
Malls remain dominant social and retail spaces in urban areas
Sari-sari (neighborhood convenience) stores serve 60%+ of FMCG distribution
Load (prepaid mobile credit) is a significant micro-economy
Debt culture: informal lending (5-6 loans) common in lower-income households
OFW (Overseas Filipino Worker) economy shapes ~22% of household consumption

regional_notes.NCR_Metro_Manila: Highest income, most urbanized, highest social media usage, cosmopolitan consumer base, 13M+ population
regional_notes.Cebu_Visayas: Second economic hub, English-dominant, growing BPO sector, distinct Visayan cultural identity
regional_notes.Davao_Mindanao: Fastest-growing region, agri-business base, younger demographic, distinct Muslim minority in western Mindanao
regional_notes.Luzon_provincial: Predominantly agricultural, strong OFW remittance economy, high religious observance

_sources: DataReportal Digital Indonesia 2024 (We Are Social / Meltwater)

income_distribution.description: Approximate share of households per income band (BPS 2023)

top_platforms_ranked: TikTok (fastest growing, dominant with Gen Z)
Twitter/X (Indonesia is one of the largest Twitter markets globally)

notes: Strong Islamic influence on purchasing — halal certification is a key trust signal
Collectivist culture with strong family and community ties, especially in Java
Price sensitivity is high; promotions, cashback, and flash sales drive purchase decisions heavily
Warung (small neighborhood shops) remain the dominant FMCG channel outside major cities
Mobile-first market — most internet access is via smartphone
GoTo (Gojek/Tokopedia) and Grab ecosystems deeply embedded in urban daily life
Aspirational consumption is strong among urban middle class — branded goods signal status
Ramadan is the single most important shopping and marketing season of the year
Rural-urban divide is significant — behavior varies greatly between Java and outer islands

regional_notes.Jakarta_Jabodetabek: Largest metro, highest income, most digitally connected, cosmopolitan and trend-setting, 30M+ people in greater area
regional_notes.Java: Most populous island (60% of national population), economic heartland, Javanese cultural norms dominant
regional_notes.Bali: Tourism economy, significant expat and foreign worker population, more liberal cultural norms, high-income tourism sector
regional_notes.Surabaya_East_Java: Second city, manufacturing and trade hub, strong Javanese-Madurese identity
regional_notes.Outer_Islands_Sumatra_Kalimantan: Natural resource economies, younger demographic, lower digital penetration, distinct ethnic identities

_sources: DataReportal Digital Singapore 2024 (We Are Social / Meltwater)
MOM (Ministry of Manpower) Singapore Labour Force Survey 2023

income_distribution.description: Approximate share of resident households by income band (SingStat 2023)

top_platforms_ranked: LinkedIn (highest professional usage rate in SEA)

notes: Highly educated, discerning consumers who research purchases thoroughly before buying
Strong preference for convenience and quality over price — time is valued highly
Multicultural society: Chinese, Malay, Indian, and expat communities have distinct preferences
Kiasu culture (fear of losing out) drives competitive consumption and FOMO-driven purchases
Health and wellness spending is among the highest in Asia
Sustainability and ethical consumption growing, especially among younger demographics
Hawker culture is central to daily life and food identity
High brand consciousness — global luxury and premium brands perform strongly
Cashless-first society — PayNow, GrabPay, PayLah widely used
Large expat population (~30% of residents) creates demand for international products

regional_notes.Central_Region_CBD: Financial and commercial hub, highest income density, professional workforce, heavy expat concentration
regional_notes.East_Region: Residential heartland for middle-class families, Changi Airport proximity, multicultural mix
regional_notes.West_Region: Industrial and tech corridor (one-north, Jurong), younger working population, HDB estates
regional_notes.North_Region: More suburban, Malay and mixed communities, older HDB towns like Woodlands and Yishun

_sources: DataReportal Digital Vietnam 2024 (We Are Social / Meltwater)

income_distribution.description: Approximate share of households per income band (GSO 2023)

top_occupations: Factory and manufacturing workers (electronics, garments, footwear)
Street food vendors and small restaurant owners
Motorbike taxi drivers (xe om) and Grab drivers
Retail shop workers and market traders

top_platforms_ranked: Facebook (dominant social platform — 93% of social media users)
YouTube (very high usage for entertainment and education)
Zalo (Vietnam-specific super-app, dominant for messaging and payments)
TikTok (fastest growing, huge Gen Z penetration)

notes: Rapid economic growth mindset — aspiration and self-improvement are core cultural drivers
Strong family values; multi-generational households common, family approval influences purchases
Zalo is essential for business and personal communication — WhatsApp equivalent but local
Price comparison is habitual — consumers regularly shop across platforms for best deals
Street food culture is deeply embedded — eating out cheaply is a daily norm
Motorbike is the primary mode of transport, shaping logistics and delivery expectations
Tet (Lunar New Year) is the dominant shopping and gifting season
Northern (Hanoi) and Southern (Ho Chi Minh City) consumers have distinct cultural styles
Growing middle class is driving demand for international brands, especially South Korean (K-beauty, Samsung)
Cash remains common outside major cities despite rapid digital payment growth (MoMo, ZaloPay)

regional_notes.Ho_Chi_Minh_City_Saigon: Economic capital, more entrepreneurial and open culture, higher income, cosmopolitan, strong startup ecosystem
regional_notes.Hanoi: Political capital, more conservative and traditional northern culture, strong civil service presence, high education levels
regional_notes.Da_Nang: Fastest-growing coastal city, tourism and tech hub, younger workforce, beach lifestyle
regional_notes.Mekong_Delta: Agricultural heartland, rice farming economy, lower incomes, strong rural traditions

_sources: DataReportal Digital Thailand 2024 (We Are Social / Meltwater)

income_distribution.description: Approximate share of households per income band (NSO 2023)

top_occupations: Street food vendors and market traders (talad)
Motorbike taxi drivers and Grab/Bolt drivers

top_platforms_ranked: LINE (essential messaging app — near-universal in Thailand)
TikTok (dominant with Gen Z and millennials)
Twitter/X (strong political and pop culture discourse)

notes: Buddhist values influence consumer mindset — mindfulness, moderation, and merit-making (tam bun) shape spending
LINE is the primary communication channel for business, customer service, and personal use — not WhatsApp
Sanuk (fun) culture: entertainment, food, and leisure are high-priority spending categories
Face (saving and giving face) is a core social dynamic — status goods and gifting are significant
Medical tourism is a major industry; health consciousness and wellness spending is high
K-pop and Korean culture have massive influence on beauty, fashion, and food trends (hallyu effect)
7-Eleven and convenience store culture is deeply embedded — Thailand has one of the highest 7-Eleven densities in the world
Songkran (Thai New Year, April) and year-end are the two peak shopping seasons
Tourism economy creates a dual market: local consumers and high-spending international visitors
Strong royal and nationalist sentiment influences brand communication norms

regional_notes.Bangkok_Greater_Metro: Primary economic and cultural hub, 17M+ in metro area, highest income, most digitally advanced, cosmopolitan
regional_notes.Chiang_Mai_North: Cultural capital of the north, large expat and digital nomad community, cooler climate lifestyle, distinct Lanna identity
regional_notes.Phuket_Samui_South: Tourism-dominated economy, seasonal income patterns, significant foreign investment, beach lifestyle
regional_notes.Northeast_Isan: Largest region by population, predominantly agricultural, lowest incomes, distinct Lao-influenced culture, high rural-urban migration to Bangkok

_template._comment: Copy this template when adding a new country. All fields optional except _sources.
